mechanism_narrative: Higher-than-expected inflation erodes real corporate earnings and tightens monetary policy expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL month-over-month change is in the top quartile over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals weakening labor demand and impending earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when the trigger fires over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of a rising federal funds rate and falling industrial production creates a stagflationary liquidity squeeze that amplifies equity downside, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2023 FEDFUNDS at 5.33% and INDPRO at 103.4886
failure_condition: Joint z-scores of FEDFUNDS and INDPRO fail to produce negative next-60-day returns in more than 45% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of restrictive monetary policy, month-over-month declines in real retail sales signal immediate demand destruction that is not yet priced into equity markets, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2023 RSAFS at 697557 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33%
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, negative month-over-month RSAFS fails to predict negative 5-day forward returns in at least 55% of triggered weeks over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and operating leverage, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when INDPRO grows month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating nonfarm payroll growth above its trailing 12-month trend signals tightening labor markets and impending wage-cost pressures that compress profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change exceeds its 12-month trailing average 3-month change
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of trigger instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer prices and declining real retail sales identifies a profit-margin squeeze where firms cannot pass input costs to consumers, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2023 CPIAUCSL at 306.269 and RSAFS at 697557
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and RSAFS fails to produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of joint-signal months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected month-over-month acceleration in the federal funds rate increases the risk-free rate and depresses the present value of future corporate cash flows, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases are followed by negative 5-day forward returns in fewer than 53% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.
